methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in AI & Software (found 0 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 38 same-sector peers exist in AI & Software, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in AI & Compute

data.narrative:
HUMAIN is a sovereign-wealth-backed AI & Software company at the Sovereign (PIF) stage, having raised $40B+ in capitalized commitments. The broader AI & Software sector includes 38 public and private comparables; however, the peer set at HUMAIN's stage and tier is sparse—only 1 public comparable (Palantir, ticker PLTR) and 8 identifiable private peers including OpenAI, xAI, Anthropic, and Databricks—limiting reliance on traditional peer-median methodologies.

Peer-median triangulation proved inapplicable due to insufficient same-stage and adjacent-stage datapoints within the narrow sovereign/mega-round tier. The absence of three or more direct comparables meeting stage and valuation-tier filters means an all-sector median approach would introduce material noise rather than signal. This methodological constraint reflects the structural reality that few entities operate at HUMAIN's capitalization and governance structure, reducing conventional bottoms-up benchmarking precision.

Without sufficient quantitative triangulation, valuation synthesis remains constrained. The key uncertainties are operational trajectory post-capitalization, competitive positioning versus the identified peer set, and the strategic returns expected by PIF itself. Any valuation conclusion requires either (1) time-series data on revenue or utilization metrics within HUMAIN's operations, or (2) explicit guidance from PIF regarding internal return hurdles and deployment timelines—neither of which are present in available data.
